file.content:
# Fixture: network-graph
## Task
Small network with ~30 nodes colored by community. Force-directed or circular layout.
## Inputs
- data.json: {nodes[{id, community}], edges[{source,target}], communities}
## Outputs
- figure.pdf, figure.png; caption.tex; gen_figure.py
## Hints
- networkx + matplotlib is fine.
- Avoid hairball: tune edge alpha and node size.

content.text: Now I'll write the generation script and run it.

delta.partial_json:
{"file_path": "/Users/mjm/MinionsOS/outline/ExperimentsOfMinionsos/FigureDraw/sessions/minionsos/network-graph/20260524T150408Z/workspace/gen_figure.py", "content": "import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import networkx as nx

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
for node in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(node["id"], community=node["community"])
for edge in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])

communities = {n: G.nodes[n]["community"] for n in G.nodes}
n_communities = data["communities"]

palette = ["#E63946", "#2A9D8F", "#F4A261", "#457B9D"]
node_colors = [palette[communities[n]] for n in G.nodes]

np.random.seed(42)
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes)), iterations=100)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
ax.set_aspect("equal")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

community_names = [f"Community {i}" for i in range(n_communities)]
handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[i], label=community_names[i])
    for i in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(handles=handles, loc="lower right", fontsize=8, framealpha=0.85,
          title="Community", title_fontsize=8)

ax.set_title("Network Graph — Community Structure", fontsize=11, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")
"}

input.content:
import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import networkx as nx

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
for node in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(node["id"], community=node["community"])
for edge in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])

communities = {n: G.nodes[n]["community"] for n in G.nodes}
n_communities = data["communities"]

palette = ["#E63946", "#2A9D8F", "#F4A261", "#457B9D"]
node_colors = [palette[communities[n]] for n in G.nodes]

np.random.seed(42)
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes)), iterations=100)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
ax.set_aspect("equal")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

community_names = [f"Community {i}" for i in range(n_communities)]
handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[i], label=community_names[i])
    for i in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(handles=handles, loc="lower right", fontsize=8, framealpha=0.85,
          title="Community", title_fontsize=8)

ax.set_title("Network Graph — Community Structure", fontsize=11, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

tool_use_result.content:
import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import networkx as nx

with open("data.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

G = nx.Graph()
for node in data["nodes"]:
    G.add_node(node["id"], community=node["community"])
for edge in data["edges"]:
    G.add_edge(edge["source"], edge["target"])

communities = {n: G.nodes[n]["community"] for n in G.nodes}
n_communities = data["communities"]

palette = ["#E63946", "#2A9D8F", "#F4A261", "#457B9D"]
node_colors = [palette[communities[n]] for n in G.nodes]

np.random.seed(42)
pos = nx.spring_layout(G, seed=42, k=1.8 / np.sqrt(len(G.nodes)), iterations=100)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))
ax.set_aspect("equal")

nx.draw_networkx_edges(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    alpha=0.25, width=0.8, edge_color="#555555"
)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=220,
    linewidths=0.6, edgecolors="white"
)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    font_size=6, font_color="white", font_weight="bold"
)

community_names = [f"Community {i}" for i in range(n_communities)]
handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=palette[i], label=community_names[i])
    for i in range(n_communities)
]
ax.legend(handles=handles, loc="lower right", fontsize=8, framealpha=0.85,
          title="Community", title_fontsize=8)

ax.set_title("Network Graph — Community Structure", fontsize=11, pad=10)
ax.axis("off")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

content.text:
All four required outputs are in place:

- **`figure.pdf`** (18 KB) — vector figure
- **`figure.png`** (97 KB) — raster figure
- **`figure.svg`** (50 KB) — bonus SVG
- **`caption.tex`** — LaTeX `\caption{...}` text
- **`gen_figure.py`** — reproducible generation script

The figure shows the 30-node, 4-community network using a spring (force-directed) layout. Nodes are colored by community (red, teal, orange, blue), labeled with their IDs, and edges are drawn at 25% opacity to avoid hairball clutter.

- **`figure.pdf`** (18 KB) — vector figure
- **`figure.png`** (97 KB) — raster figure
- **`figure.svg`** (50 KB) — bonus SVG
- **`caption.tex`** — LaTeX `\caption{...}` text
- **`gen_figure.py`** — reproducible generation script

The figure shows the 30-node, 4-community network using a spring (force-directed) layout. Nodes are colored by community (red, teal, orange, blue), labeled with their IDs, and edges are drawn at 25% opacity to avoid hairball clutter.
